NextToken.description: The pagination token to continue to the next page of results.

Contacts.description: A list of the contacts and escalation plans in your Incident Manager account.

ContactArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the contact or escalation plan.

Alias.description: The unique and identifiable alias of the contact or escalation plan.

DisplayName.description: The full name of the contact or escalation plan.

Type.description:
The type of contact.

  • PERSONAL: A single, individual contact.

  • ESCALATION: An escalation plan.

  • ONCALL_SCHEDULE: An on-call schedule.

NextToken.description: The pagination token to continue to the next page of results.

Engagements.description: A list of each engagement that occurred during the specified time range of an incident.

EngagementArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the engagement.

ContactArn.description: The ARN of the escalation plan or contact that Incident Manager is engaging.

IncidentId.description: The ARN of the incident that's engaging the contact.

NextToken.description: The token for the next set of items to return. Use this token to get the next set of results.

Rotations.description: Information about rotations that meet the filter criteria.

RotationArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the rotation.

ContactIds.description: The Amazon Resource Names (ARNs) of the contacts assigned to the rotation team.

StartTime.description: The date and time the rotation becomes active.

TimeZoneId.description: The time zone the rotation’s activity is based on, in Internet Assigned Numbers Authority (IANA) format. For example: "America/Los_Angeles", "UTC", or "Asia/Seoul".

Recurrence.description: Information about when an on-call rotation is in effect and how long the rotation period lasts.

MonthlySettings.description: Information about on-call rotations that recur monthly.

WeeklySettings.description: Information about on-call rotations that recur weekly.

DailySettings.description: Information about on-call rotations that recur daily.

NumberOfOnCalls.description: The number of contacts, or shift team members designated to be on call concurrently during a shift. For example, in an on-call schedule that contains ten contacts, a value of 2 designates that two of them are on call at any given time.

ShiftCoverages.description: Information about the days of the week that the on-call rotation coverage includes.

RecurrenceMultiplier.description: The number of days, weeks, or months a single rotation lasts.

ContactArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the created contact or escalation plan.

ContactChannelArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the contact channel.

RotationArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the created rotation.

RotationOverrideId.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the created rotation override.

ContactArn.description: The ARN of the escalation plan or contacts involved in the engagement.

Subject.description: The secure subject of the message that was sent to the contact. Use this field for engagements to VOICE and EMAIL.

Content.description: The secure content of the message that was sent to the contact. Use this field for engagements to VOICE and EMAIL.

PublicSubject.description: The insecure subject of the message that was sent to the contact. Use this field for engagements to SMS.

PublicContent.description: The insecure content of the message that was sent to the contact. Use this field for engagements to SMS.

IncidentId.description: The ARN of the incident in which the engagement occurred.

PageArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the engagement to a contact channel.

EngagementArn.description: The ARN of the engagement that engaged the contact channel.

ContactArn.description: The ARN of the contact that was engaged.

Subject.description: The secure subject of the message that was sent to the contact. Use this field for engagements to VOICE and EMAIL.

Content.description: The secure content of the message that was sent to the contact. Use this field for engagements to VOICE and EMAIL.

PublicSubject.description: The insecure subject of the message that was sent to the contact. Use this field for engagements to SMS.

PublicContent.description: The insecure content of the message that was sent to the contact. Use this field for engagements to SMS.

IncidentId.description: The ARN of the incident that engaged the contact channel.

SentTime.description: The time the engagement was sent to the contact channel.

ReadTime.description: The time that the contact channel acknowledged the engagement.

DeliveryTime.description: The time that the contact channel received the engagement.

ContactArn.description: The ARN of the contact or escalation plan.

Alias.description: The alias of the contact or escalation plan. The alias is unique and identifiable.

DisplayName.description: The full name of the contact or escalation plan.

Plan.description: Details about the specific timing or stages and targets of the escalation plan or engagement plan.

Stages.description: A list of stages that the escalation plan or engagement plan uses to engage contacts and contact methods.

RotationIds.description: The Amazon Resource Names (ARNs) of the on-call rotations associated with the plan.

ContactArn.description: The ARN of the contact that the channel belongs to.

Type.description: The type of contact channel. The type is SMS, VOICE, or EMAIL.

DeliveryAddress.description: The details that Incident Manager uses when trying to engage the contact channel.

ActivationStatus.description: A Boolean value indicating if the contact channel has been activated or not.

SimpleAddress.description:
The format is dependent on the type of the contact channel. The following are the expected formats:

  • SMS - '+' followed by the country code and phone number

  • VOICE - '+' followed by the country code and phone number

  • EMAIL - any standard email format

ContactArn.description: The ARN of the contact or escalation plan.

Policy.description: Details about the resource policy attached to the contact or escalation plan.

RotationArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the on-call rotation.

ContactIds.description: The Amazon Resource Names (ARNs) of the contacts assigned to the on-call rotation team.

StartTime.description: The specified start time for the on-call rotation.

TimeZoneId.description: The time zone that the rotation’s activity is based on, in Internet Assigned Numbers Authority (IANA) format.

Recurrence.description: Specifies how long a rotation lasts before restarting at the beginning of the shift order.

RotationOverrideId.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the override to an on-call rotation.

RotationArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the on-call rotation that was overridden.

NewContactIds.description: The Amazon Resource Names (ARNs) of the contacts assigned to the override of the on-call rotation.

StartTime.description: The date and time when the override goes into effect.

EndTime.description: The date and time when the override ends.

CreateTime.description: The date and time when the override was created.

NextToken.description: The pagination token to continue to the next page of results.

ContactChannels.description: A list of contact channels related to the specified contact.

ContactChannelArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the contact channel.

ContactArn.description: The ARN of the contact that contains the contact channel.

Type.description:
The type of the contact channel. Incident Manager supports three contact methods:

  • SMS

  • VOICE

  • EMAIL

DeliveryAddress.description: The details that Incident Manager uses when trying to engage the contact channel.

SimpleAddress.description:
The format is dependent on the type of the contact channel. The following are the expected formats:

  • SMS - '+' followed by the country code and phone number

  • VOICE - '+' followed by the country code and phone number

  • EMAIL - any standard email format

ActivationStatus.description: A Boolean value describing if the contact channel has been activated or not. If the contact channel isn't activated, Incident Manager can't engage the contact through it.

NextToken.description: The pagination token to continue to the next page of results.

ContactChannelArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the contact channel Incident Manager engaged.

ReceiptType.description: The type follows the engagement cycle, SENT, DELIVERED, and READ.

NextToken.description: The token for the next set of items to return. Use this token to get the next set of results.

PageResolutions.description: Information about the resolution for an engagement.

ContactArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of a contact in the engagement resolution process.

Type.description: The type of contact for a resolution step.

StageIndex.description: The stage in the escalation plan that resolves to this contact.

NextToken.description: The pagination token to continue to the next page of results.

Pages.description: The list of engagements to a contact's contact channel.

PageArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the page to the contact channel.

EngagementArn.description: The ARN of the engagement that this page is part of.

ContactArn.description: The ARN of the contact that Incident Manager is engaging.

IncidentId.description: The ARN of the incident that's engaging the contact channel.

SentTime.description: The time that Incident Manager engaged the contact channel.

DeliveryTime.description: The time the message was delivered to the contact channel.

ReadTime.description: The time that the contact channel acknowledged engagement.

NextToken.description: The pagination token to continue to the next page of results.

Pages.description: The list of engagements to contact channels.

PageArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the page to the contact channel.

EngagementArn.description: The ARN of the engagement that this page is part of.

ContactArn.description: The ARN of the contact that Incident Manager is engaging.

IncidentId.description: The ARN of the incident that's engaging the contact channel.

SentTime.description: The time that Incident Manager engaged the contact channel.

DeliveryTime.description: The time the message was delivered to the contact channel.

ReadTime.description: The time that the contact channel acknowledged engagement.

RotationShifts.description: Details about a rotation shift, including times, types, and contacts.

NextToken.description: The token for the next set of items to return. This token is used to get the next set of results.

ContactIds.description: The Amazon Resource Names (ARNs) of the contacts who are part of the shift rotation.

ShiftDetails.description: Additional information about an on-call rotation shift.

OverriddenContactIds.description: The Amazon Resources Names (ARNs) of the contacts who were replaced in a shift when an override was created. If the override is deleted, these contacts are restored to the shift.

RotationOverrides.description: A list of rotation overrides in the specified time range.

NextToken.description: The token for the next set of items to return. Use this token to get the next set of results.

RotationOverrideId.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the override to an on-call rotation.

NewContactIds.description: The Amazon Resource Names (ARNs) of the contacts assigned to the override of the on-call rotation.

CreateTime.description: The time a rotation override was created.

RotationShifts.description: Information about shifts that meet the filter criteria.

NextToken.description: The token for the next set of items to return. Use this token to get the next set of results.

ContactIds.description: The Amazon Resource Names (ARNs) of the contacts who are part of the shift rotation.

ShiftDetails.description: Additional information about an on-call rotation shift.

OverriddenContactIds.description: The Amazon Resources Names (ARNs) of the contacts who were replaced in a shift when an override was created. If the override is deleted, these contacts are restored to the shift.

Tags.description: The tags related to the contact or escalation plan.
